data.title: What's Going On With Airbnb Stock?
data.description:
Shares of Airbnb, Inc. (NASDAQ:ABNB) are trading higher Tuesday. The stock is seeing continued momentum following strong earnings and positive analyst revisions last week.

  • Airbnb stock is charging ahead with explosive momentum. Why is ABNB stock surging?

Upbeat Earnings Report Buoys Share Price

While the company missed earnings per share expectations, reporting $0.56 against an estimated $0.66, it surpassed revenue forecasts with $2.78 billion, compared to the anticipated $2.71 billion. This financial performance, coupled with positive reactions from analysts and strategic moves in the market, have contributed to the stock’s rise.

Analysts Get Bullish On AirBnB

Following the company’s quarterly results, multiple analysts upgraded the stock or raised price targets, which appears to be driving momentum in shares.

  • Evercore ISI Group analyst Mark Mahaney upgraded from in-line to outperform and maintained a price target of $145.
  • TD Cowen analyst Kevin Kopelman maintained a buy rating and raised the price target from $150 to $160.
  • Barclays analyst Trevor Young maintained an equal-weight rating and raised the price target from $120 to $122.
  • Wedbush analyst Scott Devitt reiterated a neutral rating and maintained a $130 price target.
  • Deutsche Bank analyst Lee Horowitz upgraded from a hold to a buy and announced a $154 price target.
  • BMO Capital analyst Brian Pitz maintained a market perform rating and raised the price target from $130 to $140.

Stock Down 20% Over Past Year

Over the past 12 months, Airbnb has seen a decline of 21.38%. The stock is currently trading at $125.75, which positions it significantly above its 52-week low of $99.88 but below the high of $163.93. This indicates that while the stock has recovered from its lowest annual points, it still has room to go before approaching its previous highs.

The technical indicators for Airbnb do not show significant movement in RSI or MACD, suggesting neutral momentum in the market. However, the stock is currently facing resistance at $138.50 and has support at $117.00. These levels are crucial for traders to watch, as a breakout or breakdown could signal future price direction.

ABNB Stock Continues To Climb

ABNB Price Action: Airbnb shares were up 3.91% at $126.09 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: 'I Was Lucky' – Comedian Ricky Gervais Says He's 'Not Proud of Being Rich' – Asks Why He's Paid a 'Million Times a Nurse's Wages'
data.description:
Comedian Ricky Gervais says he does not take pride in being rich and credits luck for his fortune.

In a 2009 interview with CBS's "60 Minutes" recently shared on the show's X page, Gervais said he was lucky to break into show business and that many people work just as hard without earning anywhere near him.

"I'm not proud of being rich," he said. "There's nothing wrong in being rich, but it's nothing to be proud of. I was lucky. Why am I paid a million times a nurse's wages?"

Don't Miss:

  • The ‘ChatGPT of Marketing' Just Opened a $0.85/Share Round — 10,000+ Investors Are Already In
  • Motley Fool's analysts have built a new lineup of passive ETFs — explore which "Foolish" strategy fits your investment goals.

‘They Don't Do What I Do'

Asked if he is "ashamed" of being wealthy, Gervais said he is not, but added that many people work just as hard and still don't make a fortune because of the kind of work they do.

"There are people who work as hard as me and they haven't got that because they don't do what I do," he told "60 Minutes." "They don't do show business. They build walls and let someone else take it."

Gervais was asked whether he'd give away all his wealth. He said he "could" but likes the benefits that come with money.

"I like the freedom it gives me," he said. "I like having a nice house."

Trending: This ETF issuer isn't chasing the index — it's building tools for income, leverage, and conviction

‘Poverty Is Horrible'

Gervais, who co-created the British sitcom "The Office," said his childhood shaped how he views money. He grew up poor and noticed that people treated him differently based on his background.

"I was poor," he told "60 Minutes." "I didn't even know I was poor. I went to university and everyone spoke differently to me and spoke like the queen. Poverty is bad, poverty is horrible."

Gervais has been open about his childhood. In a 2024 post on X, he wrote that he was raised to believe skill and ability can take you far and said he is thankful for his success.

"I had no money growing up," Gervais wrote. "My dad was a labourer and my mum did everything to make ends meet. I still felt grateful though. I’m glad I had to work for every penny I have. It feels good."

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Michael Saylor Is Telling The Truth ABout Strategy's Bitcoin Stash, Coinbase Exec Says
data.description:
John D’Agostino, Coinbase's Head of Institutional Strategy, appeared on CNBC Tuesday to weigh in on the market volatility.

D’Agostino emphasized that while the decline looks large in dollar terms, it’s typical volatility on a percentage basis for crypto.

Asked about Strategy Inc (NASDAQ:MSTR), D’Agostino, whose firm acts as a primary custodian for Strategy’s Bitcoin, said “I’ve seen no evidence that Saylor is not telling the truth,” pushing back against rumors of forced selling at the world’s largest corporate Bitcoin holder.

Speaking in November, D’Agostino noted Bitcoin has historically endured 70-80% drawdowns on the way to multi-year rallies. “If you like this asset, you should like it at a discount.”

Strategy Underwater

Strategy now holds 717,131 BTC at an average cost of roughly $76,027 per coin. With Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) trading near $68,000, the company's holdings are underwater.

Despite this, Saylor told CNBC on Feb. 10 that fears of forced liquidation are “an unfounded concern,” citing 2.5 years of cash dividends on hand to service debt.

Strategy CEO Phong Le backed this up, telling Bloomberg this week that liquidation would only trigger if Bitcoin crashed 90% and stayed there for five years—a scenario he deemed “extremely unlikely.”

Polymarket bettors currently price in a 12% chance Strategy sells any Bitcoin by year-end, with over $20 million having being wagered on the outcome.

Coinbase’s Own Struggle

D’Agostino's defense comes as his own firm faces headwinds. Coinbase's (NASDAQ:COIN) Q4 earnings missed on key metrics:

Following the print, Piper Sandler slashed its price target on COIN from $270 to $150.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Hycroft Mining Stock Drops: What's Behind The Slide?
data.description:
Hycroft Mining Holding Corp (NASDAQ:HYMC) shares are dipping on Tuesday as Lunar New Year market closures take effect across Asia on top of investor caution ahead of Wednesday's Federal Reserve meeting minutes. Here’s what you may want to know today.

  • Hycroft Mining Holding shares are sliding. Why is HYMC stock dropping?

Metals Under Pressure

The drop comes after a particularly volatile stretch for precious metals. Gold recently spiked above $5,595 an ounce before plunging back toward $4,400 in just two days at the end of January.

Fed Minutes In Focus

Traders are also staying cautious ahead of Wednesday's release of the Fed's January meeting minutes. According to Reuters, investors are hoping the minutes will offer more clarity on how policymakers are thinking as the central bank approaches a leadership transition.

President Donald Trump has nominated former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to take over when Chair Jerome Powell's term ends in May. Markets don't expect a rate move until the June 16–17 meeting, which Warsh would lead if confirmed in time.

Fed officials, including Powell, have said they expect inflation to drift back toward 2% by mid‑year, but many, including Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, want to see a clear trend in the data before acting. The Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE index, has been stuck around 2.8% since May. December PCE data arrives Friday, and officials have signaled they don't expect much improvement.

Hycroft’s Technical Data

Hycroft Mining is currently showing a bearish setup, as it is trading below key moving averages. This indicates a potential weakness in the stock’s momentum. Traders should be cautious about entering new positions at this time.

The RSI is currently neutral, which means there's no immediate overbought or oversold condition to signal a strong directional move, suggesting traders should watch for any shifts in sentiment that could push the RSI into either extreme.

MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on Hycroft Mining. This suggests that the current trend may be weakening, and traders should be alert for any signs of a potential reversal or continuation of the downtrend.

Key support is set at $31.00, and if this level is tested, it could provide a crucial point for traders to watch for a bounce or a breakdown. Conversely, without a defined resistance level, any upward movement may face challenges until a new high is established.

Hycroft Mining Holding has experienced an impressive 12-month performance of 1,095.34%, reflecting a strong longer-term trend. This substantial gain indicates that despite the current bearish setup, the stock has shown resilience and potential for recovery if momentum shifts positively.

HYMC Price Action: Hycroft Mining shares were down 7.26% at $32.97 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Dan Ives Says Apple's AI Alone Could Be Worth $1.5 Trillion — And Almost No One Is Pricing It In
data.description:
That implies Apple's AI layer alone could be worth as much as $1.5 trillion in market value (given Apple currently has about 14.7 billion shares outstanding). That’s nearly half of the company's current valuation — and yet, the market is barely pricing it in.

The disconnect helps explain why recent weakness in AAPL stock may have less to do with fundamentals and more to do with timing.

Apple's Biggest AI Advantage Is Its Installed Base

Unlike most AI companies still searching for users, Apple already has them — more than 2.5 billion active devices and over 1.5 billion iPhones worldwide. That gives Apple an unmatched distribution engine to roll out AI-powered services instantly across its ecosystem.

Ives expects Apple's long-awaited Siri overhaul and broader AI platform to begin rolling out in phases starting this summer, with more advanced capabilities arriving into 2026. The real opportunity isn't just smarter devices — it's monetization.

Apple could introduce AI-driven subscription services, embedding recurring, high-margin software revenue into its ecosystem — a shift that would move Apple deeper into SaaS-like economics.

Wall Street May Be Missing Apple's Next Growth Engine

For years, Apple's valuation has been anchored to hardware cycles. But AI has the potential to create an entirely new revenue layer on top of its existing base — without requiring Apple to sell a single additional device.

That's why Ives believes Apple's AI strategy could dramatically reshape its long-term value. The company isn't just adding AI features — it's positioning itself to monetize intelligence across the largest consumer technology ecosystem in the world.

If execution matches expectations, Apple's AI business could become one of its most valuable assets — hiding in plain sight.

Image: Shutterstock
